comments.author: @MichelleTortorici-k4y
comments.text:
As a native Texan, born and raised in San Antonio, I can say confidently that the best time to visit SA and specifically the river walk is during Christmas. The trees are covered in lights and it’s absolutely spectacular to see. Usually on Thanksgiving, there’s a parade on the river to ring on Christmas and that is absolutely stunning to see. Big Bend is great during the spring when the dogwood trees are in bloom. In my opinion, El Paso was overhyped but I’ll let y’all be the judge. The sprawling white home y’all saw in the video was actually the movie set for the show Dallas that aired in the 1980’s. It’s now a tourist attraction you can walk through with all the furnishings from the show. And a little tidbit of information, you don’t ever want to visit during the summer months. August especially unless you’re going to the beach! The heat is brutal, usually in the 100 degree area with little to no rain. Oh and the giant mechanical man you saw stands at the enters me to the Texas state fair that happens every year. 
I know this video is a few yrs old but if you make it to Fort Worth, I’d be happy to be your tour guide.

comments.text: Yo Post Person, STOP ADVERTISING TEXAS!!!!

comments.author: @Top7Daily-i9d
comments.text: Great video with a nice mix of Texas highlights! The scenery and travel tips were enjoyable, but it would be even better if you included a few hidden gems, local food spots, and the best times of year to visit each destination. A simple map or estimated travel distances could also help viewers planning a Texas road trip. Keep up the great work!

comments.author: @leejoice8744
comments.text:
I love Texas, but the reckless development destroying our trees and the insane speeding that threatens wildlife is heartbreaking. Texas is already hot enough, and cutting down all these trees for development makes it even worse. Without shade, the area becomes an oven, leading to a massive waste of energy on AC. In the end, it harms both humans and nature.

On top of that, people drive too fast, killing innocent animals every single day. Trucks and cars speed constantly. Animals cross slowly—if you just have a little patience and slow down, you can save their lives. Whole duck families get wiped out on the road. Even on country roads, people tailgate like crazy. Why are you so obsessed with speeding? Does going fast make your life any better? Please, protect our environment, slow down, and respect the wildlife.
